Brexit elasticities core results documentation

Prepare the dataset

This chunk loads the main dataset and prepares the analysis sample. First, the Excel file containing the full applicant dataset is imported (it is also pre-filtered). Non-comparable territories and aggregate categories are removed to ensure that only consistent country units remain. Two country groups are then defined: EU member states (eu_real), which represent the treated group, and a synthetic comparison set (eu_synth) used as the control pool. The dataset is restricted to observations belonging to these two groups and to years after 2015. Finally, indicator variables are created to identify whether each observation belongs to the real EU group or the synthetic comparison group, and a categorical grouping variable is constructed for convenience. Basic checks (unique countries, group counts, and data preview) are printed to verify the resulting sample.

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
data <- read_excel(
  "data_filtered.xlsx", 
  sheet = "full_data"
)

data <- data %>%
  filter(!Domicile_named_country %in% c(
    "St Barthelemy", "St Martin (French part)", "Reunion",
    "Martinique", "Guadeloupe", "Aland Islands",
    "Cyprus (Not otherwise specified)",
    "French West Indies (Not In Use)",
    "Canary Islands", "French Guiana"
  ))

eu_real <- c(
  "Austria", "Belgium", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Cyprus (European Union)",
  "Czech Republic", "Denmark", "Estonia", "Finland", "France",
  "Germany", "Greece", "Hungary",  "Italy",
  "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Luxembourg", "Malta", "Netherlands",
  "Poland", "Portugal", "Romania", "Slovakia", "Slovenia",
  "Spain", "Sweden"
)
eu_synth <- c("Australia", "Saudi Arabia", "Russian Federation", "Peru", "Kuwait", "Bahrain","Singapore","Switzerland", "Hong Kong", "United States of America",  "Thailand", "Canada", "Mauritius", "Qatar", "Norway", "Israel", "Malaysia", "Jordan", "Albania", "Serbia", "Ireland", "Jersey", "Macao","United Arab Emirates", "Vietnam [Viet Nam]", "Turkey")


data <- data %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(eu_real, eu_synth))

data_UCAS <- data %>%
  mutate(
    real_EU  = ifelse(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real, 1, 0),
    synth_EU = ifelse(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_synth, 1, 0),
    EU_group = case_when(
      Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real ~ "Real_EU",
      Domicile_named_country %in% eu_synth ~ "Synthetic_EU",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

print(unique(data$Domicile_named_country))
##  [1] "Austria"                  "Belgium"                 
##  [3] "Bulgaria"                 "Denmark"                 
##  [5] "Finland"                  "France"                  
##  [7] "Germany"                  "Greece"                  
##  [9] "Hungary"                  "Ireland"                 
## [11] "Italy"                    "Luxembourg"              
## [13] "Malta"                    "Netherlands"             
## [15] "Poland"                   "Portugal"                
## [17] "Romania"                  "Spain"                   
## [19] "Sweden"                   "Estonia"                 
## [21] "Latvia"                   "Lithuania"               
## [23] "Croatia"                  "Slovenia"                
## [25] "Czech Republic"           "Slovakia"                
## [27] "Cyprus (European Union)"  "Jersey"                  
## [29] "Russian Federation"       "Mauritius"               
## [31] "Norway"                   "Peru"                    
## [33] "Qatar"                    "Saudi Arabia"            
## [35] "Singapore"                "Switzerland"             
## [37] "Thailand"                 "United Arab Emirates"    
## [39] "Turkey"                   "United States of America"
## [41] "Vietnam [Viet Nam]"       "Serbia"                  
## [43] "Albania"                  "Australia"               
## [45] "Bahrain"                  "Canada"                  
## [47] "Hong Kong"                "Israel"                  
## [49] "Jordan"                   "Kuwait"                  
## [51] "Macao"                    "Malaysia"
table(data$EU_group)
## Warning: Unknown or uninitialised column: `EU_group`.
## < table of extent 0 >
head(data)

Next we perform a sanity check:

data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    EU_group = case_when(
      Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real  ~ "Real_EU",
      Domicile_named_country %in% eu_synth ~ "Synthetic_EU"
    )
  )
table(data$EU_group)
## 
##      Real_EU Synthetic_EU 
##          260          260

260/10 = 26 real european countries (excl. Ireland because it didnt face the same restrictions (-1) and excluding UK for obvious reasons (-1)) and 260/10=26 synthetic countries (including Ireland because it is the most plausible counterfactual)

library(plm)
## 
## Attaching package: 'plm'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     between, lag, lead
pdata_UCAS <- pdata.frame(data, index = c("Domicile_named_country", "Year"))

Saturated DID

S-DID design with DK errors

This chunk estimates a pooled difference-in-differences model using fixest::feols with Driscoll–Kraay (DK) standard errors. We convert the panel object to a plain data frame, ensure Year and country identifiers have the correct types, and define the post-treatment period (Post = 1 for years ≥ 2021) and treatment status (Treated = 1 for countries in eu_real). To allow heterogeneous post effects for each treated country while pooling all non-treated units, we build treated_factor: treated countries keep their own country label, while all controls are set to "CONTROL" (used as the reference category).

We then estimate three specifications: (1) a two-way fixed-effects model with country and year fixed effects, (2) a country fixed-effects-only model, and (3) a model without fixed effects, all using the same saturated interaction i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL"). Model summaries are printed for comparison.

The interaction term i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL") estimates a separate post-treatment effect for each treated country relative to the pooled control group. Formally, the specification can be written as

\[ \log(Applicants_{it}) = \alpha_i + \lambda_t + \sum_{c \in \mathcal{T}} \beta_c \left( \mathbf{1}\{i = c\} \times Post_t \right) + \varepsilon_{it}, \]

where \(i\) indexes countries and \(t\) indexes years, \(\alpha_i\) and \(\lambda_t\) denote country and year fixed effects, \(\mathcal{T}\) is the set of treated (EU) countries, and \(Post_t\) equals 1 for \(t \geq 2021\).
Each coefficient \(\beta_c\) captures the post-Brexit change in applications for treated country \(c\) relative to the pooled control group.

library(fixest)

# ---- clean data ----
df <- as.data.frame(pdata_UCAS)
df$Domicile_named_country <- as.character(df$Domicile_named_country)
df$Year <- as.integer(as.character(df$Year))

# ---- treatment definition ----
df$Post    <- as.integer(df$Year >= 2021)                 # post-Brexit
df$Treated <- as.integer(df$Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real)

# ---- pooled control category (NO NA on RHS!) ----
df$treated_factor <- ifelse(
  df$Treated == 1,
  df$Domicile_named_country,   # each treated country gets its own coefficient
  "CONTROL"                     # all unaffected countries pooled
)

m_sat_DK <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL") |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  vcov = "DK"
)
## Warning: The VCOV matrix is not positive semi-definite and was 'fixed' (see
## ?vcov).
summary(m_sat_DK)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Driscoll-Kraay (L=1) 
##                                               Estimate Std. Error   t value
## treated_factor::Austria:Post                 -0.704804   0.083954  -8.39509
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post                 -0.758927   0.097329  -7.79757
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post                -2.043416   0.162995 -12.53669
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post                 -1.811014   0.133594 -13.55615
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post -1.058432   0.100115 -10.57220
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post          -1.293471   0.065349 -19.79315
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post                 -1.202773   0.136265  -8.82671
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post                 -1.603714   0.147274 -10.88935
## treated_factor::Finland:Post                 -1.912616   0.237355  -8.05803
## treated_factor::France:Post                  -0.753375   0.088401  -8.52220
## treated_factor::Germany:Post                 -0.837770   0.104143  -8.04442
## treated_factor::Greece:Post                  -1.022019   0.106405  -9.60499
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post                 -1.270737   0.091312 -13.91645
## treated_factor::Italy:Post                   -1.081306   0.142580  -7.58385
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post                  -1.667786   0.148250 -11.24980
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post               -2.329383   0.302586  -7.69826
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post              -0.649165   0.071835  -9.03692
## treated_factor::Malta:Post                   -0.517949   0.050979 -10.16008
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post             -0.691242   0.050475 -13.69483
## treated_factor::Poland:Post                  -1.824551   0.166871 -10.93392
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                -1.095439   0.367180  -2.98338
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                 -1.867702   0.160261 -11.65414
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post                -1.672595   0.126135 -13.26035
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post                -1.435824   0.152012  -9.44549
## treated_factor::Spain:Post                   -0.663970   0.086524  -7.67385
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                  -1.123118   0.169847  -6.61253
##                                                Pr(>|t|)    
## treated_factor::Austria:Post                 1.5029e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post                 2.7149e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post                5.2997e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post                 2.7081e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post 2.2476e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post          9.9512e-09 ***
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post                 1.0007e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post                 1.7531e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Finland:Post                 2.0892e-05 ***
## treated_factor::France:Post                  1.3310e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Germany:Post                 2.1176e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Greece:Post                  4.9993e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post                 2.1597e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Italy:Post                   3.3830e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post                  1.3318e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post               3.0054e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post              8.2567e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Malta:Post                   3.1347e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post             2.4806e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Poland:Post                  1.6938e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                1.5365e-02 *  
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                 9.8742e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post                3.2746e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post                5.7410e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Spain:Post                   3.0819e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                  9.7855e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.179578     Adj. R2: 0.973907
##                  Within R2: 0.802428
m_sat_DK1 <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL") |
    Domicile_named_country,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  vcov = "DK"
)
## Warning: The VCOV matrix is not positive semi-definite and was 'fixed' (see
## ?vcov).
summary(m_sat_DK1)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52
## Standard-errors: Driscoll-Kraay (L=1) 
##                                               Estimate Std. Error   t value
## treated_factor::Austria:Post                 -0.525213   0.051336 -10.23088

## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post          -1.113880   0.051462 -21.64482

## treated_factor::Latvia:Post                  -1.488195   0.121586 -12.23985

## treated_factor::Malta:Post                   -0.338357   0.048807  -6.93262

## treated_factor::Poland:Post                  -1.644960   0.145599 -11.29785

## treated_factor::Hungary:Post                 1.3945e-07 ***

## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post               2.3615e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post              9.3531e-07 ***

## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post             1.0556e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Poland:Post                  1.2847e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                2.6148e-02 *  
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                 4.7544e-07 ***

## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post                2.4239e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Spain:Post                   1.6992e-04 ***
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                  7.5456e-05 ***

## RMSE: 0.197197     Adj. R2: 0.969176
##                  Within R2: 0.820902
m_sat_DK2 <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL"),
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  vcov = "DK"
)
## Warning: The VCOV matrix is not positive semi-definite and was 'fixed' (see
## ?vcov).
summary(m_sat_DK2)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Standard-errors: Driscoll-Kraay (L=1) 
##                                               Estimate Std. Error    t value
## (Intercept)                                   6.915882   0.010425 663.420219

## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post              3.3645e-11 ***

## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post             3.2298e-09 ***

## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                6.4026e-01    
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                 1.0236e-02 *  
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post                4.0169e-08 ***

## treated_factor::Spain:Post                   2.6205e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                  3.7682e-04 ***

## RMSE: 1.00887   Adj. R2: 0.276678

Event study

This chunk estimates an event-study difference-in-differences model using fixest. We define treatment status as \(EU_i=1\) for countries in eu_real and construct event time as \(\ell = t - 2020\), so \(\ell=0\) corresponds to year 2020. The regression uses i(event_time, EU, ref = 0) to estimate a full set of dynamic EU–control differences for each relative year \(\ell\), normalized to zero in the reference period \(\ell=0\). Country and year fixed effects are included, standard errors are clustered, and iplot() visualizes the estimated dynamic coefficients with \(\ell=0\) shown as the reference line. \[ \log(Applicants_{it})=\alpha_i+\lambda_t+\sum_{\ell\neq 0}\beta_{\ell}\Big(\mathbf{1}\{t-2020=\ell\}\times EU_i\Big)+\varepsilon_{it}, \]

where \(\beta_{\ell}\) traces the dynamic EU–control difference in year \(\ell\) relative to the baseline year \(\ell=0\) (2020).

Event graph

library(fixest)

df$Domicile_named_country <- as.character(df$Domicile_named_country)
df$Year <- as.integer(as.character(df$Year))

# ---- treatment definition ----
df$Post    <- as.integer(df$Year >= 2021)
df$EU      <- as.integer(df$Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real)

# ---- event-time (ℓ = t − 2020) ----
df$event_time <- df$Year - 2020

# ---- pooled EU vs control event-study DID ----
m_event <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(event_time, EU, ref = 0) |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year, 
  vcov="cluster"
)

summary(m_event)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Domicile_named_country) 
##                    Estimate Std. Error   t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## event_time::-4:EU  0.123901   0.066095   1.87458 6.6583e-02 .  
## event_time::-3:EU  0.078886   0.055947   1.41002 1.6460e-01    
## event_time::-2:EU  0.078230   0.041061   1.90523 6.2396e-02 .  
## event_time::-1:EU  0.057376   0.024217   2.36925 2.1648e-02 *  
## event_time::1:EU  -0.795767   0.086382  -9.21223 1.9598e-12 ***
## event_time::2:EU  -1.188341   0.108389 -10.96366 5.1049e-15 ***
## event_time::3:EU  -1.294385   0.124036 -10.43552 2.9551e-14 ***
## event_time::4:EU  -1.334555   0.129432 -10.31083 4.4956e-14 ***
## event_time::5:EU  -1.374155   0.132968 -10.33447 4.1513e-14 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.239724     Adj. R2: 0.955258
##                  Within R2: 0.647922
iplot(
  m_event,
  xlab = "Years relative to 2020",
  ylab = "Log difference in applications",
  ref.line = 0,
  main = "Event-study: EU vs synthetic control"
)

wald(m_event, c(
"event_time::-4:EU",
"event_time::-3:EU",
"event_time::-2:EU",
"event_time::-1:EU"
))
## Wald test, H0: joint nullity of event_time::-4:EU, event_time::-3:EU, event_time::-2:EU and event_time::-1:EU
##  stat = 1.83734, p-value = 0.120567, on 4 and 450 DoF, VCOV: Clustered (Domicile_named_country).

Interestingly, if we remove the clustering the following happens:

etable(m_event, vcov="iid")

(notice that ths standard errors are equal everywhere). Under iid (homoskedastic) errors, the event-time dummies will have the same variance in this design, and therefore identical standard errors. That follows directly from the mechanics of OLS. With iid errors, the variance of each coefficient depends only on

\[ Var(\hat{\delta}_l)=\sigma^2(X_l'X_l'^{-1}) \]

and the second moment of the regressor, and since each event-time dummy has identical support and variance after fixed effects, their estimated variances must be the same. To see this observe what happens once those dummies are explicitly defined to have heteroscedasticity consistent standard errors:

etable(m_event, vcov="hetero")

Thats generally because of the sandwich estimator that identifies the SEs in robust se design:

\[ Vcov(\beta)=(X'X)^{-1}X'\Omega X(X'X)^{-1} \] where the \(\Omega =\diag(\varepsilon_it^2)\) which is going to create some variance so chill.

Placebo graph

This chunk implements a placebo event-study to test for spurious pre-treatment dynamics. A fake treatment year is set to 2018 and event time is defined as \(\ell = t - 2018\). The specification i(event_time_pl2018, EU, ref = -1) estimates EU–control differences for each relative year while normalizing the year immediately before the placebo treatment (\(\ell=-1\)) to zero. Country and year fixed effects are included, and standard errors are clustered at the country level. The resulting coefficients are plotted to visually assess whether significant effects appear around the placebo treatment date.

\[ \log(Applicants_{it}) = \alpha_i + \lambda_t + \sum_{\ell \neq -1} \beta_{\ell} \left(\mathbf{1}\{t-2018=\ell\} \times EU_i\right) + \varepsilon_{it}, \]

where the coefficients \(\beta_{\ell}\) capture placebo EU–control differences relative to the pre-placebo year \(\ell=-1\).

# ---- placebo event time (fake Brexit in 2018) ----
df$event_time_pl2018 <- df$Year - 2018

m_placebo_2018 <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(event_time_pl2018, EU, ref = -1) |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  cluster=~Domicile_named_country
)

summary(m_placebo_2018)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Domicile_named_country) 
##                           Estimate Std. Error    t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## event_time_pl2018::-2:EU  0.045015   0.035461   1.269401 2.1006e-01    
## event_time_pl2018::0:EU  -0.000656   0.029435  -0.022285 9.8231e-01    
## event_time_pl2018::1:EU  -0.021510   0.043946  -0.489466 6.2661e-01    
## event_time_pl2018::2:EU  -0.078886   0.055947  -1.410015 1.6460e-01    
## event_time_pl2018::3:EU  -0.874654   0.106462  -8.215666 6.6947e-11 ***
## event_time_pl2018::4:EU  -1.267227   0.120941 -10.478099 2.5618e-14 ***
## event_time_pl2018::5:EU  -1.373271   0.140029  -9.807027 2.4959e-13 ***
## event_time_pl2018::6:EU  -1.413441   0.144053  -9.811977 2.4539e-13 ***
## event_time_pl2018::7:EU  -1.453041   0.146839  -9.895492 1.8432e-13 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.239724     Adj. R2: 0.955258
##                  Within R2: 0.647922
# plot
iplot(
  m_placebo_2018,
  xlab = "Years relative to fake Brexit (2018)",
  ylab = "Log difference in applications",
  ref.line = 0,
  main = "Placebo event study: fake Brexit in 2018"
)

# ---- leads-only sample (pre-2021 only) ----
df_pre <- subset(df, Year < 2021)

df_pre$event_time <- df_pre$Year - 2020

m_leads_only <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(event_time, EU, ref = 0) |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df_pre,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  cluster = ~Domicile_named_country
)

summary(m_leads_only)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 260
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 5
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Domicile_named_country) 
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## event_time::-4:EU 0.123901   0.065966 1.87826 0.066068 .  
## event_time::-3:EU 0.078886   0.055837 1.41278 0.163793    
## event_time::-2:EU 0.078230   0.040980 1.90897 0.061901 .  
## event_time::-1:EU 0.057376   0.024170 2.37390 0.021405 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.09846     Adj. R2: 0.989981
##                 Within R2: 0.040027
iplot(
  m_leads_only,
  xlab = "Years relative to 2020",
  ylab = "Log difference in applications",
  ref.line = 0,
  main = "Leads-only test without anticipation"
)

wald(m_leads_only, c(
"event_time::-4:EU",
"event_time::-3:EU",
"event_time::-2:EU",
"event_time::-1:EU"
))
## Wald test, H0: joint nullity of event_time::-4:EU, event_time::-3:EU, event_time::-2:EU and event_time::-1:EU
##  stat = 1.84456, p-value = 0.121742, on 4 and 200 DoF, VCOV: Clustered (Domicile_named_country).

Randomized treatment assignment

set.seed(123)

# ---- random placebo treatment ----
fake_EU <- sample(unique(df$Domicile_named_country),
                  length(eu_real), replace = FALSE)

df$EU_placebo <- as.integer(df$Domicile_named_country %in% fake_EU)

df$event_time <- df$Year - 2020

m_placebo_random <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(event_time, EU_placebo, ref = 0) |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  cluster=~Domicile_named_country
)

summary(m_placebo_random)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Domicile_named_country) 
##                            Estimate Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|) 
## event_time::-4:EU_placebo  0.037014   0.068210  0.542649  0.58974 
## event_time::-3:EU_placebo  0.028491   0.056921  0.500544  0.61885 
## event_time::-2:EU_placebo  0.018102   0.042469  0.426236  0.67173 
## event_time::-1:EU_placebo -0.003888   0.025554 -0.152139  0.87968 
## event_time::1:EU_placebo  -0.203167   0.139592 -1.455429  0.15168 
## event_time::2:EU_placebo  -0.200312   0.199041 -1.006385  0.31898 
## event_time::3:EU_placebo  -0.202619   0.220416 -0.919260  0.36229 
## event_time::4:EU_placebo  -0.181734   0.228602 -0.794982  0.43031 
## event_time::5:EU_placebo  -0.192875   0.235118 -0.820332  0.41584 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.400466     Adj. R2: 0.875138
##                  Within R2: 0.017465
iplot(
  m_placebo_random,
  xlab = "Years relative to 2020",
  ylab = "Log difference in applications",
  ref.line = 0,
  main = "Placebo with randomized treatment assignment"
)

Saturated event study

library(fixest)

df <- as.data.frame(pdata_UCAS)

# make sure these are plain base variables
df$Domicile_named_country <- as.character(df$Domicile_named_country)
df$Year <- as.integer(as.character(df$Year))

df$event_time <- df$Year - 2020
df$Treated <- df$Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real

df$treated_factor <- ifelse(
  df$Treated,
  df$Domicile_named_country,
  "CONTROL"
)

df$treated_factor <- factor(df$treated_factor)

# sanity checks
head(df$Domicile_named_country)
## [1] "Albania" "Albania" "Albania" "Albania" "Albania" "Albania"
head(df$treated_factor)
## [1] CONTROL CONTROL CONTROL CONTROL CONTROL CONTROL
## 27 Levels: Austria Belgium Bulgaria CONTROL Croatia ... Sweden
levels(df$treated_factor)
##  [1] "Austria"                 "Belgium"                
##  [3] "Bulgaria"                "CONTROL"                
##  [5] "Croatia"                 "Cyprus (European Union)"
##  [7] "Czech Republic"          "Denmark"                
##  [9] "Estonia"                 "Finland"                
## [11] "France"                  "Germany"                
## [13] "Greece"                  "Hungary"                
## [15] "Italy"                   "Latvia"                 
## [17] "Lithuania"               "Luxembourg"             
## [19] "Malta"                   "Netherlands"            
## [21] "Poland"                  "Portugal"               
## [23] "Romania"                 "Slovakia"               
## [25] "Slovenia"                "Spain"                  
## [27] "Sweden"
m_sat_event <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~ i(event_time, treated_factor, ref = 0, ref2 = "CONTROL") |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  vcov = ~Domicile_named_country
)
## Warning: The VCOV matrix is not positive semi-definite and was 'fixed' (see
## ?vcov).
summary(m_sat_event)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Domicile_named_country) 
##                                                         Estimate Std. Error
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Austria                  0.268438   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Belgium                  0.203324   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Bulgaria                 0.363549   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Croatia                  0.156192   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union)  0.269162   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Czech Republic          -0.067393   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Denmark                  0.202480   0.072411
## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Estonia                  0.262652   0.072411

## event_time::-4:treated_factor::Latvia                  -0.010733   0.072411

## event_time::3:treated_factor::Italy                    -0.956337   0.065321
## event_time::3:treated_factor::Latvia                   -1.895722   0.065321

## event_time::5:treated_factor::Finland                  ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::France                   ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Germany                  ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Greece                   ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Hungary                  ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Italy                    ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Latvia                   ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Lithuania                ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Luxembourg               ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Malta                    ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Netherlands              ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Poland                   ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Portugal                 ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Romania                  ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Slovakia                 ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Slovenia                 ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Spain                    ***
## event_time::5:treated_factor::Sweden                   ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.13856     Adj. R2: 0.970105
##                 Within R2: 0.882378
library(broom)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

plot_df <- broom::tidy(m_sat_event) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "^event_time::")) %>%
  mutate(
    event_time = as.integer(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+(?=:)")),
    country = str_extract(term, "(?<=treated_factor::).*"),
    conf.low = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(country), country != "CONTROL")

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = event_time, y = estimate)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    width = 0.15
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ country, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Years relative to 2020",
    y = "Estimated log effect",
    title = "Saturated DID event study by country"
  )

D-El from S-DID compared with mechanical and fee based

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

###############################################
# 1. Brexit tuition shock
###############################################

fee_pre  <- 9250
fee_post <- 22200

x <- log(fee_post/fee_pre)                     # log price change
pct_fee_change <- (fee_post - fee_pre) / fee_pre   # ≈ 1.40


###############################################
# 2. Elasticities from DiD regression
###############################################

did_df <- tidy(m_sat_DK, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("^treated_factor::", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    country = gsub("^treated_factor::", "", term),
    country = gsub(":Post$", "", country),

    beta = estimate,   # Δ log Q (DiD semi-elasticity)

    # 1) DiD quantity change (log change)
    log_change = beta,
    conf_low_log  = conf.low,
    conf_high_log = conf.high,

    # 2) DiD implied % quantity change
    pct_change_Q = exp(beta) - 1,

    # 3) Arc elasticity from DiD
    eta_arc_did = (exp(beta) - 1) / pct_fee_change,

    # 4) Structural log elasticity
    eta_log = beta / x
  ) %>%
  select(country, log_change, conf_low_log, conf_high_log,
         pct_change_Q, eta_arc_did, eta_log)


###############################################
# 3. Mechanical fee-based elasticity
###############################################

fee_df <- df %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real) %>%
  group_by(Domicile_named_country) %>%
  summarise(
    app_pre  = mean(Applicants[Year == 2020], na.rm = TRUE),
    app_post = mean(Applicants[Year == 2021], na.rm = TRUE),

    # η_mechanical = %ΔQ / %ΔP
    eta_mechanical = ((app_post - app_pre) / app_pre) / pct_fee_change,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(country = Domicile_named_country)


###############################################
# 4. Merge datasets
###############################################

plot_df <- did_df %>%
  left_join(fee_df, by = "country") %>%
  arrange(eta_log) %>%
  mutate(
    country = factor(country, levels = country),

    # small horizontal offsets so points don’t overlap
    eta_arc_shift  = eta_arc_did + 0.04,
    eta_mech_shift = eta_mechanical + 0.08
  )


###############################################
# 5. Plot
###############################################

ggplot(plot_df, aes(y = country)) +

  # zero line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +

  # confidence intervals (only structural elasticity has SEs)
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = conf_low_log, xmax = conf_high_log),
    height = 0.16,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    color = "grey45"
  ) +

  # connectors between estimates
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = eta_log, xend = eta_arc_shift, yend = country),
    color = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = eta_arc_shift, xend = eta_mech_shift, yend = country),
    color = "grey85",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +

  # (A) Structural elasticity (log derivative)
  geom_point(
    aes(x = eta_log),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.8,
    fill = "#2C6DB2",
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  # (B) DiD arc elasticity
  geom_point(
    aes(x = eta_arc_shift),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.6,
    fill = "#8FBCE6",
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  # (C) Mechanical fee elasticity
  geom_point(
    aes(x = eta_mech_shift),
    shape = 21,
    size = 2.6,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Decomposition of Post-Brexit Demand Elasticities",
    subtitle = "Dark: structural log elasticity · Light: DiD arc elasticity · Hollow: fee-based elasticity",
    x = "Demand elasticity",
    y = ""
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face="bold", size=15, margin=margin(b=6)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size=11, margin=margin(b=10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size=10),
    axis.line = element_line(linewidth=0.35),
    plot.margin = margin(10,12,10,10)
  )
## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## `height` was translated to `width`.

S-DID design DE with COVID and Post-COVID coefficients

df$Post_COVID <- as.integer(df$Year >= 2023)
df$Post <- as.integer(df$Year %in% c(2021,2022) ) 
# ---- pooled control category (NO NA on RHS!) ----
df$treated_factor <- ifelse(
  df$Treated == 1,
  df$Domicile_named_country,   # each treated country gets its own coefficient
  "CONTROL"                     # all unaffected countries pooled
)

library(fixest)

m_sat_DK_2 <- feols(
  log(Applicants) ~
    i(treated_factor, Post, ref = "CONTROL") +
    i(treated_factor, Post_COVID, ref = "CONTROL") |
    Domicile_named_country + Year,
  data = df,
  panel.id = ~ Domicile_named_country + Year,
  vcov = "DK"
)
## Warning: The VCOV matrix is not positive semi-definite and was 'fixed' (see
## ?vcov).
summary(m_sat_DK_2)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(Applicants)
## Observations: 520
## Fixed-effects: Domicile_named_country: 52,  Year: 10
## Standard-errors: Driscoll-Kraay (L=1) 
##                                                     Estimate Std. Error
## treated_factor::Austria:Post                       -0.596481   0.079975
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post                       -0.621494   0.090058
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post                      -1.849559   0.214529
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post                       -1.581933   0.117675
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post       -0.970308   0.101484
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post                -1.181364   0.081585
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post                       -0.982787   0.079094
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post                       -1.373148   0.126611
## treated_factor::Finland:Post                       -1.529000   0.158117
## treated_factor::France:Post                        -0.615964   0.084500
## treated_factor::Germany:Post                       -0.695875   0.090077
## treated_factor::Greece:Post                        -0.863994   0.083345
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post                       -1.128560   0.130103
## treated_factor::Italy:Post                         -0.869555   0.112098
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post                        -1.413352   0.171674
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post                     -1.852942   0.300219
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post                    -0.576376   0.087351
## treated_factor::Malta:Post                         -0.460216   0.042670
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post                   -0.643082   0.067719
## treated_factor::Poland:Post                        -1.527127   0.157515
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                      -0.519115   0.482188
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                       -1.607139   0.196183
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post                      -1.497592   0.134870
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post                      -1.225618   0.129349
## treated_factor::Spain:Post                         -0.514575   0.085739
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                        -0.855893   0.121245
## treated_factor::Austria:Post_COVID                 -0.777019   0.050183
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post_COVID                 -0.850549   0.050904
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post_COVID                -2.172653   0.082723
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post_COVID                 -1.963735   0.040212
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post_COVID -1.117182   0.083427
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post_COVID          -1.368209   0.026816
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post_COVID                 -1.349430   0.056141
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post_COVID                 -1.757425   0.062600
## treated_factor::Finland:Post_COVID                 -2.168361   0.103823
## treated_factor::France:Post_COVID                  -0.844983   0.033915
## treated_factor::Germany:Post_COVID                 -0.932367   0.061850
## treated_factor::Greece:Post_COVID                  -1.127369   0.074791
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post_COVID                 -1.365522   0.045840
## treated_factor::Italy:Post_COVID                   -1.222474   0.075260
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post_COVID                  -1.837409   0.021176
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post_COVID               -2.647010   0.121026
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post_COVID              -0.697690   0.047743
## treated_factor::Malta:Post_COVID                   -0.556437   0.053711
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post_COVID             -0.723349   0.040742
## treated_factor::Poland:Post_COVID                  -2.022833   0.053120
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post_COVID                -1.479654   0.110681
## treated_factor::Romania:Post_COVID                 -2.041411   0.050017
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post_COVID                -1.789264   0.109383
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post_COVID                -1.575961   0.120335
## treated_factor::Spain:Post_COVID                   -0.763567   0.041783
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post_COVID                  -1.301268   0.085843
##                                                      t value   Pr(>|t|)    
## treated_factor::Austria:Post                        -7.45835 3.8581e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post                        -6.90103 7.0588e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post                       -8.62149 1.2117e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post                       -13.44324 2.9104e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post        -9.56115 5.1920e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post                -14.48015 1.5319e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post                       -12.42555 5.7191e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post                       -10.84544 1.8138e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Finland:Post                        -9.67006 4.7276e-06 ***
## treated_factor::France:Post                         -7.28949 4.6163e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Germany:Post                        -7.72532 2.9230e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Greece:Post                        -10.36650 2.6498e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post                        -8.67433 1.1531e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Italy:Post                          -7.75712 2.8293e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post                         -8.23278 1.7589e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post                      -6.17197 1.6431e-04 ***
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post                     -6.59838 9.9460e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Malta:Post                         -10.78549 1.9004e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post                    -9.49626 5.4925e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Poland:Post                         -9.69512 4.6273e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post                       -1.07658 3.0968e-01    
## treated_factor::Romania:Post                        -8.19204 1.8304e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post                      -11.10396 1.4871e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post                       -9.47529 5.5937e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Spain:Post                          -6.00162 2.0210e-04 ***
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post                         -7.05918 5.9259e-05 ***
## treated_factor::Austria:Post_COVID                 -15.48358 8.5612e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Belgium:Post_COVID                 -16.70878 4.4054e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Bulgaria:Post_COVID                -26.26414 8.1202e-10 ***
## treated_factor::Croatia:Post_COVID                 -48.83495 3.1740e-12 ***
## treated_factor::Cyprus (European Union):Post_COVID -13.39115 3.0094e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Czech Republic:Post_COVID          -51.02242 2.1423e-12 ***
## treated_factor::Denmark:Post_COVID                 -24.03634 1.7848e-09 ***
## treated_factor::Estonia:Post_COVID                 -28.07366 4.4875e-10 ***
## treated_factor::Finland:Post_COVID                 -20.88509 6.1952e-09 ***
## treated_factor::France:Post_COVID                  -24.91454 1.2978e-09 ***
## treated_factor::Germany:Post_COVID                 -15.07465 1.0805e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Greece:Post_COVID                  -15.07356 1.0812e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Hungary:Post_COVID                 -29.78878 2.6453e-10 ***
## treated_factor::Italy:Post_COVID                   -16.24328 5.6390e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Latvia:Post_COVID                  -86.76838 1.8176e-14 ***
## treated_factor::Lithuania:Post_COVID               -21.87148 4.1195e-09 ***
## treated_factor::Luxembourg:Post_COVID              -14.61358 1.4148e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Malta:Post_COVID                   -10.35991 2.6639e-06 ***
## treated_factor::Netherlands:Post_COVID             -17.75456 2.5885e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Poland:Post_COVID                  -38.08035 2.9483e-11 ***
## treated_factor::Portugal:Post_COVID                -13.36860 3.0534e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Romania:Post_COVID                 -40.81408 1.5849e-11 ***
## treated_factor::Slovakia:Post_COVID                -16.35784 5.3033e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Slovenia:Post_COVID                -13.09645 3.6441e-07 ***
## treated_factor::Spain:Post_COVID                   -18.27451 2.0091e-08 ***
## treated_factor::Sweden:Post_COVID                  -15.15868 1.0295e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.16213     Adj. R2: 0.977372
##                 Within R2: 0.838956
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(broom)
library(stringr)

# 1) extract coefficients
coef_long <- tidy(m_sat_DK_2) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "^treated_factor::")) %>%
  mutate(
    period = case_when(
      str_detect(term, "Post_COVID$") ~ "Post-COVID",
      str_detect(term, "Post$") ~ "Post-Brexit",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    country = term %>%
      str_remove("^treated_factor::") %>%
      str_remove(":(Post|Post_COVID)$"),
    elasticity = estimate
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(period))

# 2) wide version (for ordering + segments)
plot_df2 <- coef_long %>%
  select(country, period, elasticity) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = elasticity) %>%
  arrange(desc(`Post-COVID`)) %>%
  mutate(country = factor(country, levels = unique(country)))

# 3) long version (for legend + points) 
plot_df_long <- plot_df2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`Post-Brexit`, `Post-COVID`),
    names_to = "period",
    values_to = "elasticity"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    period = factor(
      period,
      levels = c("Post-Brexit", "Post-COVID"),
      labels = c(
        "2021 tuition shock (COVID+Brexit)",
        "Persistent response (post-COVID, Brexit only)"
      )
    )
  )

# 4) plot
ggplot(plot_df_long, aes(y = country)) +

  # connecting lines (use wide data)
  geom_segment(
    data = plot_df2,
    aes(
      x = `Post-Brexit`,
      xend = `Post-COVID`,
      yend = country
    ),
    color = "grey75",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +

  # points with proper legend
  geom_point(
    aes(x = elasticity, color = period),
    size = 2.6
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("#4C78A8", "#E45756"),
    name = NULL
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Change in Demand Elasticities After Brexit",
    subtitle = "Immediate tuition shock vs persistent post-COVID response",
    x = "Demand elasticity",
    y = ""
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
  )

Robustness

Prepare the datasets

data <- read_excel(
  "data_filtered.xlsx", 
  sheet = "full_data"
)

data <- data %>%
  filter(!Domicile_named_country %in% c(
    "St Barthelemy", "St Martin (French part)", "Reunion",
    "Martinique", "Guadeloupe", "Aland Islands",
    "Cyprus (Not otherwise specified)",
    "French West Indies (Not In Use)",
    "Canary Islands", "French Guiana"
  ))


data_standard <- data %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(eu_real, eu_synth)) %>%
  mutate(
    EU = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real),
    control_group = "Synthetic"
  )

#very very poor countries
synth_poor <- c(
  "Pakistan", "Nepal", "Bangladesh", "Ethiopia",
  "Uganda", "Tanzania, United Republic of", "Rwanda", "Mozambique",
  "Malawi", "Sierra Leone",
  "Ghana", "Nigeria", "Kenya", "Zimbabwe"
)
data_poor <- data %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(eu_real, synth_poor)) %>%
  mutate(
    EU = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real),
    control_group = "Very_Poor"
  )
#very very rich countries

synth_rich <- c(
  "United States of America", "Canada", "Australia", "Norway",
  "Switzerland", "Singapore", "Qatar", "United Arab Emirates",
  "Kuwait", "Saudi Arabia", "Israel", "Japan",
  "Hong Kong"
)

data_rich <- data %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(eu_real, synth_rich)) %>%
  mutate(
    EU = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real),
    control_group = "Very_Rich"
  )

data_rich<-data_rich[data_rich$Year>2015,]
#OECD only 
oecd <- c(
  "Australia", "Canada", "Chile",
  "Colombia",
  "Iceland", "Israel", "Japan",
  "Korea, Republic of", "Mexico",
  "New Zealand", "Norway",
  "Switzerland", "Turkey",
  "United States of America"
)
data_oecd <- data %>%
  filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(eu_real, oecd)) %>%
  mutate(
    EU = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real),
    control_group = "OECD"
  )

data_oecd<-data_oecd[data_oecd$Year>2015,]
library(dplyr)
library(fixest)

# --- core helper: filter to treated + a chosen control set, then run pooled DiD ---

run_pooled_did <- function(df, treated_vec, control_vec,
                           y = "Applicants",
                           post_year = 2021,
                           vcov_type = "hetero") {

  df2 <- df %>%
    filter(Domicile_named_country %in% c(treated_vec, control_vec)) %>%
    mutate(
      EU   = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% treated_vec),
      Post = as.integer(Year >= post_year)
    )

  m <- feols(
    as.formula(paste0(
      "log(", y, ") ~ EU:Post | Domicile_named_country + Year"
    )),
    data = df2,
    vcov = vcov_type
  )

  return(list(model = m, data = df2))
}
controls_list <- list(
  "Original synthetic" = eu_synth,
  "Very poor controls" = synth_poor,
  "Very rich controls" = synth_rich,
  "OECD controls"      = setdiff(oecd, eu_real)  # keep OECD but remove treated EU from control pool
)
fits <- lapply(names(controls_list), function(nm) {
  run_pooled_did(
    df = data[data$Year>2015,],
    treated_vec = eu_real,
    control_vec = controls_list[[nm]],
    y = "Applicants",
    post_year = 2021,
    vcov_type = "hetero"     # change to "hetero" if you prefer
  )$model
})

names(fits) <- names(controls_list)
get_ate <- function(m) {
  ct <- coeftable(m)

  data.frame(
    ATE = ct["EU:Post", "Estimate"],
    SE  = ct["EU:Post", "Std. Error"],
    t   = ct["EU:Post", "t value"],
    p   = ct["EU:Post", "Pr(>|t|)"],
    N   = nobs(m),
    R2_within = as.numeric(fitstat(m, "wr2"))
  )
}

ATE_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(fits, get_ate))
ATE_table$Spec <- names(fits)
ATE_table <- ATE_table[, c("Spec","ATE","SE","t","p","N","R2_within")]

print(ATE_table)
##                                  Spec       ATE         SE         t
## Original synthetic Original synthetic -1.265119 0.04698451 -26.92630
## Very poor controls Very poor controls -1.671866 0.07184766 -23.26960
## Very rich controls Very rich controls -1.325263 0.05519092 -24.01234
## OECD controls           OECD controls -1.255891 0.05530483 -22.70853
##                               p   N R2_within
## Original synthetic 1.993022e-96 520 0.6128571
## Very poor controls 4.785608e-73 400 0.6284359
## Very rich controls 2.742817e-75 390 0.5873253
## OECD controls      8.238646e-71 400 0.5633532

TWFE DIDs for different controls

ATE_table <- ATE_table[
  match(
    c("Very poor controls",
      "Original synthetic",
      "Very rich controls",
      "OECD controls"),
    ATE_table$Spec
  ),
]

# sanity check
stopifnot(!any(is.na(ATE_table$Spec)))

# ---- confidence intervals ----
ATE_table$lower <- ATE_table$ATE - 1.96 * ATE_table$SE
ATE_table$upper <- ATE_table$ATE + 1.96 * ATE_table$SE

# ---- numeric y positions ----
y_pos <- seq_len(nrow(ATE_table))

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(ATE_table, aes(x = ATE, y = Spec)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  xlab("ATE (log points)") +
  ylab("")
## `height` was translated to `width`.

Bootstrapped ATE

library(fixest)
library(dplyr)

set.seed(123)

eu_countries     <- intersect(unique(data$Domicile_named_country), eu_real)
non_eu_countries <- setdiff(unique(data$Domicile_named_country), eu_real)

B <- 1000
boot_ATE <- rep(NA_real_, B)

for (b in 1:B) {

  boot_eu  <- sample(eu_countries, length(eu_countries), replace = TRUE)
  boot_neu <- sample(non_eu_countries, length(non_eu_countries), replace = TRUE)

  boot_countries <- c(boot_eu, boot_neu)

  df_b <- data %>%
    filter(Domicile_named_country %in% boot_countries) %>%
    mutate(
      EU   = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real),
      Post = as.integer(Year >= 2021)
    )

m_b <- suppressMessages(
  suppressWarnings(
    feols(
      log(Applicants) ~ EU : Post |
        Domicile_named_country + Year,
      data = df_b,
      fixef.rm = "perfect"
    )
  )
)

  if ("EU:Post" %in% names(coef(m_b))) {
    boot_ATE[b] <- coef(m_b)["EU:Post"]
  }
}

boot_ATE_clean <- boot_ATE[!is.na(boot_ATE)]

# diagnostics
length(boot_ATE_clean) / B    # should be high (e.g. > 0.9)
## [1] 1
# inference
quantile(boot_ATE_clean, c(0.025, 0.975))
##      2.5%     97.5% 
## -1.412975 -1.113521
sd(boot_ATE_clean)
## [1] 0.07822566
mean(boot_ATE_clean)
## [1] -1.269757
#Option 1
hist(
  boot_ATE_clean,
  breaks = 40,
  main = "Stratified cluster bootstrap: Brexit ATE",
  xlab = "EU × Post coefficient",
  freq=FALSE
)
abline(v = 0, lty = 2)
abline(v = mean(boot_ATE_clean), col = "red")

#Option 2
lattice::densityplot(
  ~ boot_ATE_clean,
  plot.points = FALSE,
  auto.key = list(columns = 2),
  lwd = 2, 
  xlab = "EU × Post coefficient",
  main = "Stratified cluster bootstrap: Brexit ATE"
)

Callaway–Sant’Anna DR for different controls

library(dplyr)
library(did)

run_cs <- function(dat, eu_vec = eu_real){

  dat2 <- dat %>%
    mutate(
      EU = as.integer(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_vec),
      id = as.numeric(factor(Domicile_named_country)),
      t  = Year,
      g  = ifelse(EU == 1, 2021, 0),
      log_app = log(Applicants)   # use log1p(Applicants) if you have zeros
    )

  att <- att_gt(
    yname  = "log_app",
    tname  = "t",
    idname = "id",est_method = "dr",
    gname  = "g",
    data   = dat2,
    panel  = TRUE
  )

  list(
    simple  = aggte(att, type = "simple"),
    dynamic = aggte(att, type = "dynamic")
  )
}

# --- run on each synthetic donor pool ---data_standard
res_st <- run_cs(data_standard[data_standard$Year>2015,])
res_poor <- run_cs(data_poor[data_poor$Year>2015,])
res_rich <- run_cs(data_rich)
res_oecd <- run_cs(data_oecd)

# --- print overall ATT ---
summary(res_st$simple)
## 
## Call:
## aggte(MP = att, type = "simple")
## 
## Reference: Callaway, Brantly and Pedro H.C. Sant'Anna.  "Difference-in-Differences with Multiple Time Periods." Journal of Econometrics, Vol. 225, No. 2, pp. 200-230, 2021. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001>, <https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09015> 
## 
## 
##      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  
##  -1.1974        0.1059     -1.405     -0.9898 *
## 
## 
## ---
## Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0
## 
## Control Group:  Never Treated,  Anticipation Periods:  0
## Estimation Method:  Doubly Robust
summary(res_poor$simple)
## 
## Call:
## aggte(MP = att, type = "simple")
## 
## Reference: Callaway, Brantly and Pedro H.C. Sant'Anna.  "Difference-in-Differences with Multiple Time Periods." Journal of Econometrics, Vol. 225, No. 2, pp. 200-230, 2021. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001>, <https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09015> 
## 
## 
##      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  
##  -1.4782        0.1564    -1.7847     -1.1717 *
## 
## 
## ---
## Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0
## 
## Control Group:  Never Treated,  Anticipation Periods:  0
## Estimation Method:  Doubly Robust
summary(res_rich$simple)
## 
## Call:
## aggte(MP = att, type = "simple")
## 
## Reference: Callaway, Brantly and Pedro H.C. Sant'Anna.  "Difference-in-Differences with Multiple Time Periods." Journal of Econometrics, Vol. 225, No. 2, pp. 200-230, 2021. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001>, <https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09015> 
## 
## 
##      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  
##  -1.2343        0.1218     -1.473     -0.9956 *
## 
## 
## ---
## Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0
## 
## Control Group:  Never Treated,  Anticipation Periods:  0
## Estimation Method:  Doubly Robust
summary(res_oecd$simple)
## 
## Call:
## aggte(MP = att, type = "simple")
## 
## Reference: Callaway, Brantly and Pedro H.C. Sant'Anna.  "Difference-in-Differences with Multiple Time Periods." Journal of Econometrics, Vol. 225, No. 2, pp. 200-230, 2021. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001>, <https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09015> 
## 
## 
##      ATT    Std. Error     [ 95%  Conf. Int.]  
##  -1.1865        0.1254    -1.4324     -0.9406 *
## 
## 
## ---
## Signif. codes: `*' confidence band does not cover 0
## 
## Control Group:  Never Treated,  Anticipation Periods:  0
## Estimation Method:  Doubly Robust
# --- plot event-study style ATT ---
ggdid(res_st$dynamic)  

ggdid(res_poor$dynamic)  # poor donor pool

ggdid(res_rich$dynamic)  # rich donor pool

ggdid(res_oecd$dynamic)  # OECD donor pool

print("Baseline control group")
## [1] "Baseline control group"
nrow(data_standard[data_standard$Year>2015,])
## [1] 520
print("Number of entities")
## [1] "Number of entities"
length(unique(data_standard[data_standard$Year>2015,]$Domicile_named_country))
## [1] 52
print("vector lenght") 
## [1] "vector lenght"
length(eu_synth)
## [1] 26
print("baseline vector lenght:") 
## [1] "baseline vector lenght:"
length(eu_real)
## [1] 26
print("poor control group")
## [1] "poor control group"
nrow(data_poor[data_poor$Year>2015,])
## [1] 400
print("Number of entities")
## [1] "Number of entities"
length(unique(data_poor[data_poor$Year>2015,]$Domicile_named_country))
## [1] 40
print("vector lenght") 
## [1] "vector lenght"
length(synth_poor)
## [1] 14
print("baseline vector lenght:") 
## [1] "baseline vector lenght:"
length(eu_real)
## [1] 26
print("rich group")
## [1] "rich group"
nrow(data_rich)
## [1] 390
print("Number of entities")
## [1] "Number of entities"
length(unique(data_rich$Domicile_named_country))
## [1] 39
print("vector lenght") 
## [1] "vector lenght"
length(synth_rich)
## [1] 13
print("baseline vector lenght:") 
## [1] "baseline vector lenght:"
length(eu_real)
## [1] 26
print("oecd group")
## [1] "oecd group"
nrow(data_oecd)
## [1] 400
print("Number of entities")
## [1] "Number of entities"
length(unique(data_oecd$Domicile_named_country))
## [1] 40
print("vectoentities")
## [1] "vectoentities"
length(oecd)
## [1] 14
print("baseline vectorentities")
## [1] "baseline vectorentities"
length(eu_real)
## [1] 26

Omitted-variable sensitivity — sensemakr (Cinelli–Hazlett)

library(sensemakr)
## See details in:
## Carlos Cinelli and Chad Hazlett (2020). Making Sense of Sensitivity: Extending Omitted Variable Bias. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B (Statistical Methodology).
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Post_2021 = Post + Post_COVID,
    EU = ifelse(Domicile_named_country %in% eu_real, 1, 0)
  )

m <- lm(
  log(Applicants) ~ EU:Post_2021 + as.factor(Domicile_named_country) + as.factor(Year),
  data = df
)


summary(m) # shows the estimated coefficient
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log(Applicants) ~ EU:Post_2021 + as.factor(Domicile_named_country) + 
##     as.factor(Year), data = df)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.93122 -0.17337  0.01019  0.17406  1.27725 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                           Estimate Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                                4.72932    0.09249
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Australia                 1.70635    0.11979
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Austria                   1.94272    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Bahrain                   1.26833    0.11979
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Belgium                   2.49673    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Bulgaria                  2.56646    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Canada                    3.11488    0.11979
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Croatia                   0.88451    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Cyprus (European Union)   3.33538    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Czech Republic            2.29694    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Denmark                   1.54326    0.12207
## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Estonia                   1.11585    0.12207

## as.factor(Domicile_named_country)Vietnam [Viet Nam]        11.166  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(Year)2017                                        -0.358 0.720844    
## as.factor(Year)2018                                         0.549 0.583286    
## as.factor(Year)2019                                         0.640 0.522732    
## as.factor(Year)2020                                         0.751 0.453232    
## as.factor(Year)2021                                         5.681 2.39e-08 ***
## as.factor(Year)2022                                         3.328 0.000947 ***
## as.factor(Year)2023                                         3.010 0.002756 ** 
## as.factor(Year)2024                                         2.829 0.004867 ** 
## as.factor(Year)2025                                         2.201 0.028243 *  
## EU:Post_2021                                              -26.926  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.2679 on 458 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.9573, Adjusted R-squared:  0.9517 
## F-statistic: 168.5 on 61 and 458 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
sensemakr(m, treatment = "EU:Post_2021")
## Sensitivity Analysis to Unobserved Confounding
## 
## Model Formula: log(Applicants) ~ EU:Post_2021 + as.factor(Domicile_named_country) + 
##     as.factor(Year)
## 
## Null hypothesis: q = 1 and reduce = TRUE 
## 
## Unadjusted Estimates of ' EU:Post_2021 ':
##   Coef. estimate: -1.26512 
##   Standard Error: 0.04698 
##   t-value: -26.9263 
## 
## Sensitivity Statistics:
##   Partial R2 of treatment with outcome: 0.61286 
##   Robustness Value, q = 1 : 0.69493 
##   Robustness Value, q = 1 alpha = 0.05 : 0.66998 
## 
## For more information, check summary.

To assess the robustness of the estimated treatment effect to omitted variable bias, we apply the sensemakr framework. This approach evaluates how strong an unobserved confounder would need to be, in terms of its residual association with both the treatment and the outcome, to alter the substantive conclusions. We construct the sensitivity object. Let \(U\) denote a hypothetical omitted confounder. The sensitivity analysis evaluates the partial \(R^2\) of \(U\) with the outcome conditional on the treatment and observed covariates,

\[ R^2_{Y \sim U \mid X,D} \]

and the partial \(R^2\) of \(U\) with the treatment conditional on observed covariates,

\[ R^2_{D \sim U \mid X} \]

where \(D = EU_i \times Post2021_t\) is the treatment interaction and \(X\) includes the country and year fixed effects.

The robustness value summarizes the minimum strength that an omitted confounder must have, jointly with the treatment and outcome, to overturn the inference. This quantity represents the smallest combination of \(R^2_{Y \sim U \mid X,D}\) and \(R^2_{D \sim U \mid X}\) that would render the treatment effect statistically insignificant.

We also compute the robustness value required to reduce the point estimate to zero:

Setting \(q = 1\) asks how strong an omitted confounder would need to be to fully explain away the estimated effect, i.e.

\[ \hat{\beta}_1^{adjusted} = 0 \]

The sensitivity plot visualizes combinations of partial \(R^2\) values for a hypothetical omitted confounder that would be sufficient to attenuate the estimate or its statistical significance.

library(sensemakr)

m <- lm(log(Applicants) ~ EU:Post_2021+as.factor(Domicile_named_country)+as.factor(Year), data = df)

# Sensitivity object (optional, for plots etc.)
s <- sensemakr(m, treatment = "EU:Post_2021")

# Robustness value: pass the MODEL (m), not s
robustness_value(m, treatment = "EU:Post_2021")

robustness_value(m, treatment = "EU:Post_2021", q = 1)  # for "reduce estimate to 0"
